name: Philippines
summary: The Philippines is a Southeast Asian mixed-legal-system unitary republic combining Spanish civil-law substantive heritage with American common-law procedural inheritance. Family law operates under the Family Code of the Philippines (Executive Order 209/1987) for non-Muslim matters and the Code of Muslim Personal Laws (Presidential Decree 1083/1977) for Muslim matters heard by Shari'a District Courts. Parental authority is governed by Family Code arts. 209-233; joint exercise during marriage is the statutory default with parental authority over the child of unwed parents lodged in the mother. The Philippines does not have divorce — it operates annulment, legal separation, and recently the Family Court framework with Republic Act 8369/1997 Family Courts Act. The Supreme Court (Korte Suprema) is the apex court for civil, criminal, constitutional, and administrative matters. Psychology profession is regulated under the Psychology Act of 2009 (Republic Act 10029) establishing the Professional Regulatory Board of Psychology under the Professional Regulation Commission. Philippines is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the welfare-of-the-child standard. Philippines acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 March 2016.

statutory_framework.title: Family Code — Parental authority
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute on family law. Arts. 209-233 govern parental authority; joint exercise during marriage is the statutory default. The Philippines does not have divorce — annulment and legal separation operate as alternatives.

statutory_framework.title: Family Courts Act 1997
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute establishing specialised Family Courts with jurisdiction over family-law matters.

statutory_framework.title: Psychology Act of 2009
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute regulating the psychology profession. Establishes the Professional Regulatory Board of Psychology under the Professional Regulation Commission. Statutory title protection for psychologist and psychometrician.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of the Philippines
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil, criminal, constitutional and administrative matters.

professional_regulators.name: Professional Regulatory Board of Psychology (PRBP) under Professional Regulation Commission (PRC)
professional_regulators.role: Statutory regulatory board for psychologists under RA 10029. Operates licensing examinations and disciplinary jurisdiction.

professional_regulators.name: Psychological Association of the Philippines (PAP)
professional_regulators.role: Peak professional association for psychologists in the Philippines.

anonymisation_convention: Philippine family-law decisions of higher courts (CA, SC) are typically published with party identities; family-court decisions at trial level use anonymisation. SC decisions in family matters frequently use initials per AM No. 02-11-10-SC.

key_developments.title: Philippines independence + Manuel Roxas + Third-Republic framework
key_developments.description: Philippines independence 4 July 1946 from the United States — Manuel Roxas first president of independent Third Republic. Substantively distinctive Southeast Asian US-territorial-independence framework. Foundational pre-Marcos framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Marcos Martial Law + 21-year-authoritarian framework
key_developments.description: Ferdinand Marcos Martial Law declaration 23 September 1972 — substantively distinctive Southeast Asian 14-year-authoritarian framework 1972-1986. Substantial Marcos-era political-institutional framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-modernisation.

key_developments.title: EDSA Revolution + Cory Aquino + democratic-restoration framework
key_developments.description: EDSA People Power Revolution 22-25 February 1986 ending Marcos era. Cory Aquino first female president 1986-1992 — substantively distinctive Southeast Asian Catholic-democratic-restoration framework. Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory affecting subsequent family-law-modernisation.

key_developments.title: Constitution 1987 + Family Code (EO 209)
key_developments.description: Constitution of the Philippines 1987 adopted 2 February 1987 by referendum substantively reforming political-institutional framework. Executive Order 209 of 1987 enacted the Family Code in force 3 August 1988 substantively significant family-law-codification framework.

key_developments.title: Philippines ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Philippines ratified the UNCRC on 21 August 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration applied through Family Code 1987 + Code of Muslim Personal Laws + customary-law framework.

key_developments.title: Family Courts Act (RA 8369)
key_developments.description: Specialised Family Courts established 28 October 1997 with jurisdiction over family-law matters. Substantively significant family-law-judicial-framework consolidation within Asian framework.

key_developments.title: Psychology Act (RA 10029)
key_developments.description: Statutory psychology profession regulation enacted 16 March 2010 (signed 2 March 2009) establishing Professional Regulatory Board of Psychology (PRBP) under Professional Regulation Commission. Substantive evaluator-quality-anchor for court-appointed psychological assessments.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession + Duterte presidency framework
key_developments.description: Philippines acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 on Child Abduction effective 1 March 2016 — substantively significant Asian Hague-accession framework. Rodrigo Duterte inaugurated 30 June 2016 substantively distinctive Southeast Asian populist-authoritarian framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Mental Health Act RA 11036 + statutory mental-health framework in family-court context
key_developments.description: Mental Health Act (RA 11036) signed 20 June 2018 — substantively significant Filipino mental-health-statutory framework. Substantive Mental Health Act framework establishing Philippine Council for Mental Health + statutory recognition of mental-health as human-right + relevant to PA-cases involving mental-health-allegations against parents + child-mental-health-protection framework + foundation for subsequent 2024 Supreme Court welfare-of-the-child substantive register integrating mental-health framework. Structurally distinctive globally first Southeast Asian comprehensive Mental Health Act framework.

key_developments.title: Marcos Jr. presidency + dynastic-return framework
key_developments.description: Bongbong Marcos (Ferdinand Marcos Jr.) inaugurated 30 June 2022 — substantively distinctive Southeast Asian Marcos-dynastic-return framework 36 years after 1986 EDSA Revolution. Sara Duterte vice-president. Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory affecting subsequent family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Duterte ICC investigation + cooperation-framework + family-law-impact context
key_developments.description: International Criminal Court (ICC) Pre-Trial Chamber I authorisation 26 January 2023 resuming investigation of former President Rodrigo Duterte's war-on-drugs framework 2016-2022 — substantively distinctive globally only modern ICC investigation of former-Philippine-President framework + Marcos Jr. administration declining ICC cooperation framework 2023 + structurally distinctive Marcos-Duterte coalition fracture 2024 framework + substantively significant institutional context affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory + Supreme Court of the Philippines jurisprudence + Family Code 1987 framework implementation + welfare-of-the-child substantive register evolution + foundation for subsequent 2024 Absolute Divorce Bill framework.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court of the Philippines — welfare-of-the-child substantive register + Absolute Divorce Bill framework
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of the Philippines continues to develop welfare-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Code 1987 arts. 209-233 + Family Courts Act + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes. Absolute Divorce Bill (House Bill 9349) passed House of Representatives 22 May 2024 — substantively distinctive Philippines globally only-major-jurisdiction-without-divorce-civil-framework potentially reform-trajectory affecting subsequent family-law-modernisation. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Philippines operates a structurally distinctive mixed-legal-system framework — Spanish civil-law substantive heritage + American common-law procedural inheritance + Muslim personal-law parallel courts (Shari'a District Courts under PD 1083/1977 Code of Muslim Personal Laws). Within the corpus's mixed-jurisdiction cluster.

No-divorce statutory regime is structurally distinctive — Philippines is among the last jurisdictions globally without civil divorce. Annulment + legal separation operate as alternatives within Family Code framework.
PRBP/PRC statutory regulatory board places Philippines among the federal-statutory psychology regulator group within the corpus.
Hague Convention 1980 accession 2016 places Philippines in the Hague cluster.
editorial_notes: Philippines jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Philippines-independence-to-Marcos-Jr trajectory: 1946-Philippines-independence-+-Manuel-Roxas-+-Third-Republic + 1972-Marcos-Martial-Law-+-21-year-authoritarian + 1986-EDSA-Revolution-+-Cory-Aquino-+-democratic-restoration + 1987-Constitution-+-Family-Code-EO-209 + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1997-Family-Courts-Act-RA-8369 + 2009-Psychology-Act-RA-10029 + 2016-Hague-Convention-1980-accession-+-Duterte-presidency + 2022-Marcos-Jr-presidency-+-dynastic-return + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-Absolute-Divorce-Bill.

Mixed-legal-system framework (Spanish civil-law substantive + American common-law procedural + Muslim personal-law parallel courts). Family Code 1987 + Family Courts Act 1997 + RA 10029 PRBP + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2016. No civil divorce.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive welfare-of-the-child analysis under Family Code 1987 arts. 209-233 + Family Courts Act + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Asian + Southeast-Asian + mixed-jurisdiction-Spanish-civil-law-substantive-+-American-common-law-procedural-+-Muslim-personal-law-Shari'a-District-Courts-PD-1083-1977 + federal-statutory-psychology-regulator-PRBP-PRC-RA-10029 + Marcos-Martial-Law-1972-1986-+-EDSA-People-Power-Revolution + Cory-Aquino-first-female-president-1986-1992 + Marcos-Jr-dynastic-return-2022-36-years-after-EDSA + Duterte-populist-authoritarian-2016-2022 + only-major-jurisdiction-without-divorce-civil-framework-globally-distinctive + Absolute-Divorce-Bill-House-Bill-9349-2024 + Hague-Convention-1980-accession-2016 clusters within the corpus.
